runtime_understanding.runtime_summary: This repository is a Python-based documentation-generation service composed of (1) a FastAPI backend that scans local Python projects, generates a structured "understanding" using an LLM, compares scans, and builds/stores standalone HTML developer documentation; and (2) a Streamlit UI that calls the backend to trigger scans and to attach runtime/tooling context (text blocks + screenshots). The backend uses local filesystem workspace paths to save scans, understandings and generated documents; it supports multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic/Claude, etc.) via a provider factory. Screenshots provided show the Streamlit app running on port 8501 and the backend producing HTTP request logs for /api/projects/... endpoints.

tooling_stack.purpose: Primary implementation language for backend and UI.
tooling_stack.evidence: Project files are Python modules (app/*, ui/streamlit_app.py). Code-scan statistics show many python modules, classes and functions.

tooling_stack.purpose: HTTP API backend exposing project scan, compare, understand, and document-generation endpoints.
tooling_stack.evidence: app/api/project_routes.py contains many router.post/get endpoints (e.g. POST /api/projects/documentation/sync, /scan, /{project_name}/understand). app.main defines routes '/' and '/health'.

tooling_stack.purpose: Runs the FastAPI application in development or production.
tooling_stack.evidence: Terminal screenshot shows 'Application startup complete.' followed by many INFO lines of HTTP requests (typical Uvicorn access logs).

tooling_stack.purpose: Frontend UI used by developers to trigger documentation sync, attach runtime context blocks, and upload screenshots.
tooling_stack.evidence: ui/streamlit_app.py module present; terminal screenshot shows 'streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py' and the Streamlit server messages including 'Local URL: http://localhost:8501'.

tooling_stack.purpose: Streamlit app uses requests to call FastAPI endpoints (sync and sync-with-context).
tooling_stack.evidence: ui.streamlit_app functions call requests.post and requests.get (parse_json_response, sync_documentation, sync_documentation_with_context).

tooling_stack.purpose: Stores scan JSON, understandings, generated HTML, and runtime assets/screenshots.
tooling_stack.evidence: Documented storage paths in services: workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json and workspace/understandings/<project_name>/<understanding_id>.json; DocumentStorage docstring mentions storing HTML and runtime assets.

tooling_stack.purpose: Generate narrative project 'understanding' and analyze runtime/screenshot context with vision-capable LLMs.
tooling_stack.evidence: app.services.llm.openai_provider, app.services.llm.anthropic_provider, app.services.llm.factory, and usage in project_routes endpoints (get_llm_provider, get_llm_api_key, get_llm_model, project_understanding_service.generate_understanding, runtime_context_analyzer).

runtime_flow.title: User triggers documentation sync from the UI
runtime_flow.description: Developer opens the Streamlit UI and calls the synchronization action (either the all-in-one documentation/sync endpoint or the sync-with-context endpoint that accepts context blocks and screenshots). The Streamlit UI builds a multipart form payload (if screenshots present) and posts to the FastAPI backend.
runtime_flow.evidence: ui/streamlit_app.py contains sync_documentation and sync_documentation_with_context functions that POST to /api/projects/documentation/sync and /api/projects/documentation/sync-with-context. Terminal screenshot 2 shows 'streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py' and Streamlit accessible at localhost:8501.

runtime_flow.title: Backend scans the local project
runtime_flow.description: The FastAPI endpoint calls ProjectScanner.scan_project which lists files, parses Python modules via PythonCodeParser, computes hashes and project statistics, and returns a stored scan object.
runtime_flow.evidence: app.api.project_routes sync endpoints call project_scanner.scan_project; app.services.project_scanner and app.services.code_parser exist with docstrings describing scanning/parsing behavior. project_routes called_functions include project_scanner.scan_project and scan_storage.save_scan.

runtime_flow.title: Scan is saved to scan storage (workspace)
runtime_flow.description: The scan result is saved to local ScanStorage under workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json and the endpoint records the scan metadata.
runtime_flow.evidence: app.services.scan_storage.ScanStorage docstring describes save location. project_routes code calls scan_storage.save_scan(scan_result=scan_result).

runtime_flow.title: If a previous document exists: compare scans
runtime_flow.description: If a latest document exists for the project, the backend recovers previous runtime assets (screenshots) if necessary, obtains the previous runtime context, and uses ScanComparator to compare the old and new scans to determine if documentation-relevant changes exist.
runtime_flow.evidence: project_routes uses document_storage.list_documents, _recover_runtime_assets_for_document, _get_runtime_context_from_document, and scan_comparator.compare_scans. app.services.scan_comparator exists and project_routes calls comparison_result.get('summary', {}).

runtime_flow.title: Generate LLM 'understanding' and analyze runtime context
runtime_flow.description: When changes exist (or for initial creation), the backend builds an LLM-ready context and calls the configured LLM provider via ProjectUnderstandingService.generate_understanding; separately, RuntimeContextAnalyzer may analyze supplied screenshots and user-written blocks, producing structured runtime context that gets merged into the document metadata.
runtime_flow.evidence: project_routes calls _generate_and_save_understanding (calls project_understanding_service.generate_understanding via get_llm_provider/get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model). RuntimeContextAnalyzer class is present, and project_routes calls _analyze_runtime_context_with_llm(stored_scan=..., runtime_context=...).

runtime_flow.title: Build HTML documentation and save document artifact
runtime_flow.description: DocumentBuilder builds a standalone HTML developer documentation page from the stored understanding and the runtime context; DocumentStorage saves the HTML and associated metadata (including runtime context and asset references).
runtime_flow.evidence: project_routes calls DocumentBuilder.build_html and document_storage.save_document. app.services.document_builder.DocumentBuilder and app.services.document_storage.DocumentStorage exist with docstrings describing HTML generation and storage of runtime assets.

runtime_flow.title: Streamlit UI opens generated HTML or returns metadata
runtime_flow.description: After a successful sync, the backend returns a DocumentationSyncResponse with document_id and metadata. The Streamlit UI uses build_document_url to open the /api/projects/{project_name}/documents/{document_id}/html endpoint (FileResponse) or display operation results in the UI.
runtime_flow.evidence: ui.streamlit_app.build_document_url builds a URL using normalize_api_url and the API path; project_routes has GET /{project_name}/documents/{document_id}/html which returns FileResponse built from document_storage.get_document_file.

runtime_flow.title: Runtime assets handling and merging
runtime_flow.description: When the user supplies runtime context blocks and screenshots, the backend saves uploaded screenshot files to a workspace assets batch, builds a runtime_context object linking blocks to screenshot assets using screenshot_index, and merges with any existing runtime context from previous documents (deduping screenshots and blocks).
runtime_flow.evidence: project_routes.sync_project_documentation_with_context calls _save_uploaded_screenshots_to_temp, document_storage.prepare_runtime_assets, _build_runtime_context_from_blocks. There are helper functions in project_routes for _merge_runtime_contexts, _merge_screenshot_lists, _merge_context_blocks, and signatures/deduplication functions such as _screenshot_signature and _context_block_signature.

screenshot_insights.title: Backend access logs showing API activity
screenshot_insights.what_it_shows: Terminal shows Uvicorn/ASGI access logs with repeated GET requests to /api/projects/AutoDocX/documents and a POST to /api/projects/documentation/sync returning '200 OK'. Also shows 'Application startup complete.'
screenshot_insights.why_it_matters: Confirms the FastAPI backend is running and serving the documented API endpoints; demonstrates the Streamlit UI (or another client) is successfully calling the backend to list documents and to trigger documentation synchronization.

screenshot_insights.title: Streamlit UI startup and port
screenshot_insights.what_it_shows: Terminal shows the exact command used to run the UI: 'streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py' and the Streamlit server messages including 'Local URL: http://localhost:8501' and 'Network URL: http://192.168.43.184:8501'.
screenshot_insights.why_it_matters: Confirms the repository includes a Streamlit app and how developers run it. The UI serves on port 8501 and will call the backend endpoints (so developer must ensure backend is running and that the API base URL configured in the UI points to the FastAPI server).

operational_notes: Confirmed: The Streamlit frontend is run with 'streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py' (screenshot shows this command and the Streamlit server on port 8501).

Confirmed: The backend exposes many project-related REST endpoints (see app/api/project_routes.py), including POST /api/projects/documentation/sync and POST /api/projects/documentation/sync-with-context which accept uploaded screenshots and ordered runtime context blocks.

Not visible from the provided context: The exact command used to start the FastAPI backend process. Typical invocation is 'uvicorn app.main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000' but that exact command is not present in the scanned files or screenshots.

Required environment variables: LLM_PROVIDER and provider-specific API keys are required (app.core.config.get_llm_provider, get_llm_api_key, get_llm_model). The repository expects these to be set in the environment or a .env file.
Workspace directories are created by app.core.config.create_required_directories and the storage implementation expects write access to these workspace paths (scans, understandings, documents).

Streamlit build_context_blocks_payload uses screenshot_index to match uploaded files to context blocks. The provided user payload metadata shows two context blocks and two uploaded PNG images (screenshot_001.png, screenshot_002.png).

risks_or_gaps.title: Backend start command not documented in repo scan
risks_or_gaps.description: The code scan shows FastAPI app definitions and Uvicorn logs were observed in the screenshot, but there is no explicit top-level run script or README entry extracted by the scanner that documents how to start the backend. A new developer may not know which host/port or launch options to use.

risks_or_gaps.title: Missing or undocumented environment variable requirements (LLM keys, provider selection)
risks_or_gaps.description: LLM_PROVIDER and provider-specific environment variables are validated in app.core.config.get_llm_provider/get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model and will raise ValueError if missing or invalid. The necessary variables and example .env are not visible in the provided context.

risks_or_gaps.title: Runtime storage is filesystem-only and needs directory permissions
risks_or_gaps.description: Document, scan, and understanding storage all use local workspace paths. If the workspace path is not writable or not created, runtime errors can occur. create_required_directories exists but it must be invoked before operations or the server/startup path must call it.

risks_or_gaps.title: Potential privacy/security risk from screenshot uploads
risks_or_gaps.description: Screenshots uploaded via sync-with-context can contain sensitive PII. There is no evidence of sanitization, redaction, or access controls in the provided scan; stored runtime assets may be accessible from generated HTML or filesystem if not protected.

risks_or_gaps.title: LLM calls may be rate-limited or incur costs
risks_or_gaps.description: ProjectUnderstandingService and RuntimeContextAnalyzer call external LLM providers. Without quota/keys control, generation may fail or incur unexpected costs. Also error handling maps provider errors to HTTP 502; integration tests/configuration are advisable.

risks_or_gaps.title: Unclear default backend port and CORS/origin setup for Streamlit integration
risks_or_gaps.description: Streamlit UI runs on port 8501, but the backend API base URL used by the UI is built at runtime (normalize_api_url). The default API host/port expected by the UI is not visible; CORS configuration (to allow the UI to call the API from browser) is not shown in the excerpt.

risks_or_gaps.title: No visible authentication/authorization
risks_or_gaps.description: API endpoints appear to accept uploads and trigger LLM operations; there is no evidence in the scanned routes of authentication or fine-grained authorization. If deployed beyond a trusted local environment, this is a security gap.
